goal: Rebuild analysis phases: Leiden communities, Jaccard+git coupling, dead code detection, flow tracing, and impact analysis

tasks.name: Leiden community detection
microSteps: Write failing tests: detect_communities returns CommunityDetectionResult with Leiden-quality partitions (higher modularity than label propagation)
Implement community.py using igraph + leidenalg: build igraph.Graph from storage CALLS/IMPORTS/EXTENDS edges, run leidenalg.find_partition with ModularityVertexPartition
Create COMMUNITY nodes in storage, assign MEMBER_OF relationships from symbol nodes to community nodes
Return CommunityDetectionResult with communities list and modularity score
tasks.action: Replace label propagation with Leiden algorithm via igraph + leidenalg. Build an igraph.Graph from the KuzuDB relationships (CALLS, IMPORTS, EXTENDS weighted edges). Run leidenalg.find_partition(graph, leidenalg.ModularityVertexPartition). Store communities as COMMUNITY nodes with MEMBER_OF relationships.

tasks.name: Coupling analysis with Jaccard + git history
microSteps: Write failing tests: compute_coupling returns CouplingResult with both structural Jaccard similarity and git co-change coupling scores
Implement structural coupling: Jaccard similarity of shared callers/callees between symbol pairs
Implement git history coupling: parse git log to find files that change together in commits, compute temporal co-change frequency
Combine scores: max(jaccard, git_coupling) or weighted average for COUPLED_WITH relationship properties
Create COUPLED_WITH relationships in storage for pairs above threshold
tasks.action: Rebuild coupling.py with dual scoring: (1) Jaccard similarity of shared callers/callees from graph, (2) git history co-change coupling via subprocess git log parsing. Combine both signals into COUPLED_WITH relationships with structural_score and temporal_score properties. Git coupling captures patterns that pure structural analysis misses.

tasks.name: Dead code, flows, and impact phases
microSteps: Write failing tests for dead_code: symbols with zero incoming CALLS/IMPORTS edges (excluding entry points and exported symbols) flagged as dead
Implement dead_code.py: query storage for unreferenced symbols, return DeadCodeResult list
Write failing tests for flows: trace execution paths from entry points via forward CALLS edges
Implement flows.py: BFS from entry point nodes through CALLS edges, return FlowResult with path chains
Write failing tests for impact: BFS blast radius from changed files
Implement impact.py: impact_analysis(storage, file_path, max_depth) returns ImpactResult with affected nodes at each depth

failingVerify: python3 -m pytest tests/test_context_dead_code.py tests/test_context_flows.py tests/test_context_impact.py -x 2>&1 | tail -3
passingVerify: python3 -m pytest tests/test_context_dead_code.py tests/test_context_flows.py tests/test_context_impact.py -x 2>&1 | tail -3

tasks.action: Rebuild three analysis phases. Dead code: Cypher query for symbols with zero incoming CALLS/IMPORTS edges (exclude entry points and exported symbols). Flows: BFS from entry point nodes through forward CALLS edges, tracing execution paths. Impact: BFS blast radius from all symbols in a changed file, expanding through CALLS/IMPORTS/EXTENDS edges up to max_depth.
verify: python3 -m pytest tests/test_context_dead_code.py tests/test_context_flows.py tests/test_context_impact.py -v 2>&1 | tail -10

planVerify: python3 -m pytest tests/test_context_community.py tests/test_context_coupling.py tests/test_context_dead_code.py tests/test_context_flows.py tests/test_context_impact.py -v 2>&1 | tail -10
commitMessage: feat(context-graph): analysis phases (Leiden communities, coupling, dead code, flows, impact)
